Patch Set 1:

It surprised me that liblustre does not need libz for the Adler32 checksum?  The should be a default checksum algorithm available for all clients.

Patch Set 1:

libcfs/libcfs/posix/posix-adler.c:unsigned long zlib_adler32(...) has our own version of the alder checksum.

Patch Set 1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

These build changes seem ok to me.
Patch Set 1: Looks good to me, approved

Your change could not be merged due to a path conflict.

Please merge (or rebase) the change locally and upload the resolution for review.

Patch Set 1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

can you please rebase this?

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

ZLIB is conditional. No need to remove it from the make files.

Patch Set 1:

Did some more digging. You are correct. We can remove zlib completely. libcfs implements its own zlib_adler32 in posix-adler.c.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 7:

Depending on availability, these tools should be linking with libreadline. Does that not require libncurses?

Patch Set 7: Code-Review+1

I found the following:

lustre/include/linux/obd.h:147
#if defined(__KERNEL__) && !defined(HAVE_ADLER)
/* zlib_adler() is an inline function defined in zutil.h */
#define HAVE_ADLER
#endif

lustre/include/darwin/obd_cksum.h:30
#if !defined(__KERNEL__) && defined(HAVE_ADLER)
#  include <zlib.h>
#endif /* !__KERNEL__ */

So, it looks was for a Darwing support only.

Could you also remove those instances?

Patch Set 7:

(1 comment)

1 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 7:

Tested Patch Set 7 and saw the following warning:

dpkg-shlibdeps: warning: couldn't find library liblustreapi.so needed by debian/lustre-tests/usr/lib/lustre/tests/ll_dirstripe_verify (ELF format: 'elf64-x86-64'; RPATH: '')

>ldd debian/lustre-tests/usr/lib/lustre/tests/ll_dirstripe_verify                                                 
        linux-vdso.so.1 =>  (0x00007fff7177d000)
        libpthread.so.0 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpthread.so.0 (0x00007f79b610d000)
        liblustreapi.so => not found
        libc.so.6 => /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libc.so.6 (0x00007f79b5d81000)
        /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2 (0x00007f79b6333000)

>ll_dirstripe_verify: error while loading shared libraries: liblustreapi.so: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory

>dh_shlibdeps -l <path> seems to be ignored whatever path is specified. It works however by setting LD_LIBRARY_PATH=lustre/utils
Uploaded patch set 8.

Patch Set 8:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/23051/

Patch Set 8:

(1 comment)

1 style warning(s).
For more details please see https://wiki.hpdd.intel.com/display/PUB/Coding+Guidelines

Patch Set 8:

The fully qualified path in "dh_shlibdeps -l $(CURDIR)/lustre/utils" solved the problem.

There is however, another issue:
The finally built packages have filename, e.g.
lustre-utils_2.4.0-1_amd64.deb, where version 2.4.0-1 is extracted from
debian/changelog [lustre (2.4.0-1)] and this version does not depend on the true Lustre version.

I think it would be a good idea
to extract the version e.g. as follows:

LUSTRE_VERSION=$(echo `git describe` | sed -e "s/_/\./g" | cut -c2-)

and update the entry by means of command "dch" in debian/rules such that build Debian packages have correct filenames

Patch Set 8: Verified+1

http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/23051/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 9: Code-Review-1

Needs to be rebased.

Patch Set 9:

I think we should abandon this patch and start over. I built
yesterday successfully server and client on Debian Wheezy with the original 3.2 Debian kernel, however, ZFS as backend only. For that
I modified debian/rules and set in configure --disable-ldiskfs and
set proper spl/zfs configure settings in debian/rules. Moreover, with the command
"dpkg-buildpackage" the kernel modules are not build. One first needs
to install the deprecated package: "module-assistant", then to set
the variable: export MODULE_LOC=path_to_lustre_sources, then goto the kernel source, e.g. cd /usr/src/linux, and finally, execute
"make-kpkg modules_image". I don't know whether there is an easier way to build the kernel modules, however, that is not elegant at all.

Patch Set 9:

Insteadd of abandoning this patch we should reduce the scope. The next patch should just aim to cleanup the unlink dependencies. The debian build issues can be handled in another ticket. What you think about that suggestion Thomas?

Patch Set 9:

That is a good idea, I will remove the Debian part from this patch and resubmit the cleanup and unlink dependencies only.

parents.subject: LU-4800 obdclass: runtime load lustre client when needed

commit.subject: LU-3382 build: clean unused link dependencies
commit.message:
LU-3382 build: clean unused link dependencies

Compiled executable Lustre files such as:
lst, lstclient, loadgen, lustre_rsync, lctl, lfs,
debugctl and ptlctl are linked against libtinfo.so.5
and libncurses.so.5, however none of the library's
symbols are used. Same issue with multiop which is
linked against librt.so.1, however uses none of the
library's symbols, and liblustre.so which is linked
against libz.so.1 and uses none of the library's
symbols as well.

Signed-off-by: Thomas Stibor <thomas@stibor.net>
Signed-off-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I861878ca93dc0a07e2d4d718870a960eb86974a9
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/6427
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
